message:
fix(cargo_path_deps): kill & reap `cargo metadata` on timeout instead of leaking the child

Previously, `invoke_cargo_metadata` handed the child process
ownership to a waiter thread (`std::thread::spawn(move ||
child.wait_with_output())`) and polled `handle.is_finished()` in a
loop against a deadline. On timeout the function returned a
`MetadataInvocationFailure` error and dropped the thread handle —
but the thread kept running, still owning the child, still reading
its stdout pipe. The `cargo metadata` process was never killed, so
a wedged invocation leaked a process per timeout and pinned
stdout-pipe memory until the child eventually exited on its own
(or was reaped by the kernel on parent death).

Restructure around `child.try_wait()` so the parent keeps control
of the `Child` handle:

  - On the happy path the loop sees `Ok(Some(_))` from `try_wait()`
    (process exited) and breaks, then reads stdout/stderr via
    `child.wait_with_output()` exactly as before.
  - On timeout, the parent now:
      1. Calls `child.kill()` to send SIGKILL, best-effort;
      2. Reaps with a bounded 2s polling loop of `try_wait()`
         (`sleep(20ms)` between polls) so we don't leave a zombie
         nor hang indefinitely on a process that somehow ignores
         SIGKILL;
      3. Attaches `kill_failed=<err>` and/or `reap_failed=true`
         diagnostics to the returned error so a caller debugging a
         persistent cargo-metadata problem can tell whether the
         cleanup path itself was the victim.
  - On poll error (`try_wait` itself failing), surface a clean
    `MetadataInvocationFailure` naming the manifest.

Error kind, message, and the existing `timeout_secs=<n>` diagnostic
are preserved so upstream fallback-to-manifest-parsing behavior is
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
